raw_proposal.background: Molecular graphs can be serialized to integers. A Gödel-style prime encoding promises an injective, recoverable mapping.
raw_proposal.goal: Demonstrate an injective Gödel-style encoding of molecular graphs on a designed test set (unique integer per non-isomorphic molecule).
raw_proposal.method: Canonically label each graph (Morgan-style refinement), encode atoms and bonds as prime-power products packed into one integer, and verify zero collisions plus exact round-trip decode over the test set.
raw_proposal.expected_output: A unique integer per non-isomorphic molecule and a decode that recovers the canonical graph (injectivity on the tested sample).

hypotheses.statement: Molecule graphs admit an injective Gödel-style encoding on the tested set

decision_rule.expression: collision_count == 0 over the test set => support (injective on the tested set); collision_count > 0 => refute

hypotheses.non_circularity: the generator emits molecular graphs with no guarantee of distinct codes, so collisions could occur; the verifier independently checks the encoding for collisions over the generated set -- a zero count is therefore informative, not a property baked into the generator

target_claims.statement: An injective encoding exists on the tested set
